body: Do you think vinyl is actually better than digital or is it just nostalgia? I'm considering getting a turntable.
The ritual of putting on a record is special. Sound-wise, a well-mastered digital file beats most vinyl setups.
I love vinyl for the artwork and liner notes. It makes you listen to a whole album instead of shuffling.
Good points. What turntable would you recommend for a beginner? Don't want to spend a fortune.
Audio-Technica LP120 is the sweet spot. Built-in preamp, solid build quality, and around $250-300.
